text: Right upper lobe of the lung was adhered to the anterior metastinal mass.
Wedge resection of right upper lobe was first performed with an endoscopic stapler.
Mobilization of the thymus was started at the anterior portion of the right phrenic nerve.
It was continued parallel to the right phrenic nerve and the superior vena cava.
Next, the thymus was dissected off the pericardium from the right lower pole of thymus while moving superiorly towards the mass.
The thymus was also completely mobilized medially by dividing the mediastinal pleura interiorly along the length of the internal mammary artery and vein at the level of the sternum.
Clear evidence of pericardial involvement was identified near the inferior portion of the mass.
So, the pericardium was in sight for en bloc resection.
The venous branches from the innominate vein were controlled by the robotic small clip applier.
We open the left mediastinal pleura and enter the left thoracic space.
An additional 5 mm thoracoscope was introduced to directly visualize the left frantic nerve.
This image was directly and simultaneously visualized by the surgeon as an additional window within the robotic console using dedicated software.
Thus, dissection of the left thymus was performed parallel to the left ceramic nerve under direct vision.
The pericardial defect measured approximately 10 cm by 10 cm.
The pericardium was reconstructed with a 0.1 mm vortex membrane using 3-0 polypropylene suture in a running fashion.
